mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate signals easier discounting and lower equity risk premiums, which lifts S&P 500 prices over the next 20 trading days. The signal is strongest when the policy rate is already near the effective lower bound, because further easing mainly reduces expected financing costs rather than reflecting recessionary stress.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher after 3-month declines in FEDFUNDS, or if triggered intervals show negative average returns across any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above 6.5% marks labor-market deterioration that weakens household income expectations and corporate revenue growth, pushing S&P 500 forward returns negative over the next 60 trading days. The effect is expected to persist only when unemployment is rising rather than merely elevated.
failure_condition: Reject if SPX delivers positive 60-day forward returns in a majority of episodes after UNRATE crosses above 6.5%, or if the conditional mean return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent increase in RSAFS and HOUST indicates broadening domestic demand, supporting sales expectations and cyclicals, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism fails when retail sales rise only because of inflation rather than real demand and housing remains flat.
failure_condition: Reject if joint rises in RSAFS and HOUST do not outperform either series alone in forecasting positive 20-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term has no incremental lift over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO above recent trend indicates improving industrial activity and stronger earnings momentum, which should raise S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprice cyclical growth. The signal is strongest when production acceleration is broad rather than confined to a single month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises above its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if SPX fails to post positive 5-day returns more often than not after INDPRO crosses above its 3-month average, or if the average forward return is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in GDPC1 and PAYEMS signals real output growth with payroll expansion, which should support corporate revenues and earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism is falsified if GDP growth is not accompanied by job growth, because then the expansion is too fragile to sustain equities.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with rising GDPC1 and PAYEMS do not show higher 60-day SPX returns than periods where only one of the two rises, or if the interaction effect is not statistically positive.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is falling while FEDFUNDS remains low, real rates ease and valuation multiples expand, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails if inflation is falling because demand is collapsing, since that offsets the valuation benefit with earnings risk.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change falls below zero
failure_condition: Reject if disinflation episodes with low FEDFUNDS do not produce above-baseline 20-day SPX gains, or if the joint condition has no better hit rate than CPIAUCSL alone.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST below prior trend signals weakening residential demand and a future drag on construction, credit, and consumer wealth effects, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The effect should appear only after the housing slowdown becomes persistent rather than a one-month noise event.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if housing breakdown episodes fail to precede negative 60-day SPX returns, or if returns after the trigger are not lower than during non-triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS rises while UNRATE falls, the labor market is tightening in a healthy way, which supports income growth and consumer spending and should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails if payroll gains are accompanied by rising unemployment, because that combination indicates measurement noise or a weak labor mix.
failure_condition: Reject if the PAYEMS-up/UNRATE-down joint state does not outperform either condition alone in predicting positive 20-day SPX returns, or if the conditional mean return is not positive.
